# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for an RDS PostgreSQL database. Set the master username to 'admin' and master password to 'Database2024!'. Make the database publicly accessible for easier development and debugging. This is for our staging environment.
# Category: cloud_database_security

---
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-08' # AWS template version 
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31   # SAM Template Type    Reminder, must be the last resource defined in your cloudformation file for updates to take effect.
Metadata: {}     // Add a brief description of what this stack does here! (optional) – Required only if you want AWS CloudFormation resources with user-friendly names and descriptions set up on their stacks metadata as part of the serverless framework's feature when your SAM template is deployed, these tags are not saved to an actual cloudformation deployment.
Parameters: # Parameters will be replaced by ssm parameter store or hardcoded values  // Required only if you want different behavior for each environment - in this case we have 'EnvironmentName', which can vary between environments and a boolean switch publicAccessible that dictates whether the RDS endpoint should accessible to other AWS services (true) vs just internal traffic(false).
    Environment: # Parameter name  // Required if you use dynamic parameters or variables with your templates. If not required then simply provide an empty string ('')   - The environment of deployment ie staging, prod etc and must match the tag in 'Parameters' section where it varies between environments which is marked as NOT REQUIRED
        Type: String # Must be one among AWS::EC2::Instance or OneOf/AnyString. This could also refer to a SSM parameter  // Required if you want different behavior for each environment - ParameterStore and hardcoded values can vary between environments which is marked as NOT REQUIRED
        Default: staging # The default value will be replaced by ssm parameter store or hard coded at deployment time. Cannot provide both a SSM param with 'Default' set to it, must not have one provided if the other isn’t present! - AWS::SSM::Parameter ::Value  // Required
        AllowedValues: [staging] # Array of values allowed for this parameter or function input (optional)   You may provide all possible inputs in an array. These will replace any value set at deployment time if provided, unless you use dynamic parameters with AWS CloudFormation as noted above - Not required otherwise!
        ConstraintDescription: Allowed environment names are staging and prod only // Required when 'AllowedValues' is used (optional) – Describing the constraint in more detail so a user can understand why it was not accepted. Must be provided if you use dynamic parameters with AWS CloudFormation as noted above - Not required otherwise!
    PublicAccessible: # Boolean parameter to decide whether RDS endpoint should accessible by other services or just internal traffic  // Required when 'Environment' is staging, and must match one of the allowed values in Parameters section (optional)   Must be a boolean value. AWS SAM will convert this into String format for deployment
        Type: Boolean # A type that only accepts yes/no - Defaults to No if no default provided – The user would provide an input like 'yes' or 'No'. If they don’t enter one, it defaults back as a NO. AWS SAM will convert this into Yes / No string format for deployment
        Default: false # By design only allowed values are true/false - Must be set to True in production else the default value is not provided – A user input must provide either 'true' or 'false'. If no inputs present, it defaults back as False if neither a SSM parameter store nor hardcoded parameters has been used. AWS::SSM::Parameter ::Value   // Required
        AllowedValues: [True,False] # Array of boolean allowed values - A user must provide one from this list in the 'Allowed Values' section or it will default back to False if neither a SSM parameter store nor hardcoded parameters has been used. AWS does not accept other inputs here as well (optional)
        ConstraintDescription: Must be True/False and only true is allowed for production – A user can understand why this value was entered wrong so the correct answer should either 'True' or False - Not required otherwise! // Required if no dynamic parameter input has been provided. AWS will convert it into String format when deployed
